name: ADBC (Arrow Database Connectivity)

auth.notes: Auth method depends on specific driver (PostgreSQL, SQLite, Snowflake, BigQuery, etc.)

performance.notes: Zero-copy Arrow data transfer; significantly faster than ODBC/DBAPI for bulk data

agent_readiness.notes: Apache Arrow-native database connectivity. Zero-copy bulk data transfer between databases and Arrow format. pip install adbc-driver-postgresql adbc-driver-sqlite adbc-driver-snowflake adbc-driver-flightsql. import adbc_driver_postgresql.dbapi as pg. conn = pg.connect('postgresql://localhost/mydb'). cursor = conn.cursor(). cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM table'). table = cursor.fetch_arrow_table(). → pyarrow.Table (zero-copy). fetch_df() for pandas. DBAPI2 compatible interface. Bulk ingestion: cursor.adbc_ingest('tbl', arrow_table). Available drivers: PostgreSQL, SQLite, Snowflake, DuckDB, BigQuery, Flight SQL. Much faster than psycopg2/sqlalchemy for bulk analytics. Young API — not all drivers production-ready.

gotchas: Young project — not all drivers are production-stable
Driver quality varies significantly between databases
Requires Apache Arrow familiarity for full benefit
DBAPI2 interface means standard SQL only — no ORM layer
best_when: Bulk data extraction from databases to Arrow/Pandas/Polars
Analytics pipelines needing zero-copy database → DataFrame
When pyarrow is already in the stack and ODBC overhead is too high
avoid_when: When driver for your DB is not yet production-stable
Small result sets where ODBC overhead is negligible
summary: ADBC provides Apache Arrow-native database connectivity. import adbc_driver_postgresql.dbapi as pg; conn = pg.connect(uri); cursor = conn.cursor(); cursor.execute(sql); table = cursor.fetch_arrow_table(). Zero-copy Arrow transfer — much faster than psycopg2 for bulk analytics. Drivers: PostgreSQL, SQLite, Snowflake, DuckDB, BigQuery, FlightSQL. Apache-2.0. Young project — driver maturity varies. Best for analytics pipelines. Use SQLAlchemy for OLTP.
